title: thinking::has_ultrathink_keyword whole-string lowercase per scan; allocate-per-call hot path
description:
## Severity
Trivial
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/thinking.rs:32-39, 68-75 (has_ultrathink_keyword, has_ultrathink_in_messages)
## Violation
```rust
pub fn has_ultrathink_keyword(text: &str) -> bool {
    let lower = text.to_lowercase();
    if find_whole_word(&lower, "ultrathink") { return true; }
    ULTRATHINK_PHRASES.iter().any(|p| lower.contains(p))
}
```

Called on every user message scan, on every turn, for every provider. `text.to_lowercase()` allocates a full copy of the message — typical user message is small but turns with pasted log dumps or file contents can be 100KB+. Combined with `has_ultrathink_in_messages` iterating ALL messages on every prompt invocation, the cost is O(N · M) allocations per turn where N is messages and M is total bytes.

`find_whole_word` then performs `haystack[start..].find(needle)` in a loop — fine — but operates on the lowercase copy, doubling memory live during the scan.
## Mandated refactor
Use case-insensitive matching without allocation: walk the haystack byte-by-byte comparing `b.to_ascii_lowercase()` against the needle bytes. Or use the `memchr` crate's case-insensitive search. Or hoist the lowercase outside the loop (`has_ultrathink_in_messages` should lowercase each message ONCE and pass the lowercased slice to `has_ultrathink_keyword`). For very large messages, limit the scan to the first/last 4KiB — the keyword convention is "say it in the prompt", not "hide it in pasted logs".
## Filed from QA review dispatch
